quotes.text: Because I could not stop for Death – He kindly stopped for me –
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I felt a Funeral, in my Brain, and Mourners to and fro...
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I heard a Fly buzz – when I died –
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: After great pain, a formal feeling comes – The Nerves sit ceremonious, like Tombs –
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: There's a certain Slant of light, Winter Afternoons – That oppresses, like the Heft Of Cathedral Tunes –
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Pain — has an Element of Blank — It cannot recollect When it begun — or if there were A time when it was not —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: It was not Death, for I stood up, And all the Dead, were out –
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The Soul has Bandaged moments — When too appalled to stir —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The Heart asks Pleasure — first — And then — Excuse from Pain — And then — those little Anodynes That deaden suffering —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: To die — takes just a little while — They say it doesn't hurt —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I measure every Grief I meet With narrow, probing, eyes — I wonder if It weighs like Mine — Or has an Easier size —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: A Death blow is a Life blow to some Who till they died, did not alive become —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Parting is all we know of heaven, and all we need of hell.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I died for Beauty — but was scarce Adjusted in the Tomb When One who died for Truth, was lain In an adjoining Room —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: They say that 'Time assuages' — Time never did assuage — An actual suffering strengthens, As sinews do, with age —
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: That it will never come again is what makes life so sweet.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Forever is composed of nows.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: To live is so startling it leaves little time for other occupations.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The soul should always stand ajar, ready to welcome the ecstatic experience.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: If I can stop one heart from breaking, I shall not live in vain.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: A wounded deer leaps highest.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Saying nothing sometimes says the most.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Success is counted sweetest by those who ne'er succeed.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Truth is so rare, it is delightful to tell it.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I dwell in possibility.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Pardon my sanity in a world insane.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Unable are the loved to die, for love is immortality.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The Brain is wider than the Sky.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Find ecstasy in life; the mere sense of living is joy enough.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: There is no Frigate like a Book to take us Lands away.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The morning light is the best, it is so fresh and clean.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: They might not need me; yet they might. I'll let my heart be just in sight.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Nature is a haunted house--but Art--is a house that tries to be haunted.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Behavior is what a man does, not what he thinks, feels, or believes.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Dogs are better than human beings because they know but do not tell.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Beauty is not caused. It is.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: We turn not older with years, but newer every day.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: This is my letter to the world that never wrote to me.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I'm nobody! Who are you? Are you nobody, too?
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Heart! We will forget him! You and I tonight!
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: To love and be loved is to feel the sun from both sides.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Until I loved, I never lived.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The truth must dazzle gradually, or every man be blind.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Not knowing when the dawn will come I open every door.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: A word is dead when it is said, some say. I say it just begins to live that day.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Hold dear to your parents, for it is a scary and confusing world without them.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I non-exist without you.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: I know nothing in the world that has as much power as a word.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: To write is to carve a path through the wilderness of the mind.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: How strange that nature does not knock, and yet does not intrude!
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The soul selects her own society, then shuts the door.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: The world is not conclusion; a species stands beyond.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Morning is when I am awake and there is a dawn in me.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: To hold is not to possess; to love is to set free.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: If I read a book and it makes my whole body so cold no fire can warm me, I know that is poetry.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Death is a wild night and a new road.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: There is always a land of beyond.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Sweet is the swamp with its secrets.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: But a book is only the heart's picture-book.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: To be alive is power.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Earth is a heaven, if we only had the eyes to see it.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Where you are, that is home.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: One need not be a chamber to be haunted; one need not be a house.

quotes.text: Love is its own rescue; for we, at our best, are but love.

quotes.text: The heart wants what it wants - or else it does not care.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Nature is our eldest mother, she never tells her secrets.

quotes.text: The past is not a package one can lay away.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson
